name: Signal Lost
description: What if the wifi never failed? No dead spots, no six-minute silences — just a clean, endless connection. And without the constraint, the thing between them never quite ignited. The story of how too much access killed the spark.

divergence_event.description: The onboard wifi, for once, doesn't drop. A clean signal the whole way. The forced pauses that made every message matter never happen.

images.narrative_caption: The router held all night. Clean signal, coast to coast. And that was exactly the problem.
No four-minute windows. No six-minute deaths. Just full bars and infinite, weightless time.
'where are you headed. cool. yeah I needed to get away too. ha.' Easy. Meaning nothing.
'nice talking to you,' she typed, and meant it exactly as much as it sounds.
Somewhere a router worked perfectly, and because of it, nothing happened at all.
